seo.defaultDescription: Docassist helps clinicians and patients track medications, adherence, questionnaires, and safety signals in one secure dashboard.

login.subtitle: Sign in to continue to your secure workspace.

validation.invalid: Invalid email or password. Please try again.

setupAccount.title: Set a permanent password
setupAccount.subtitle: This one-time step finishes onboarding for {email}.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Medication tracking extends the authenticated shell with a shared record: doctors assign active medications, and patients log whether each medication was actually taken.
dashboard.doctorWorkflowDescription: Open the roster to manage patient access, assign medications, and review adherence.
dashboard.doctorWorkspaceSummary: The roster now acts as the doctor workspace for onboarding, medication assignment, and recent adherence review.
dashboard.patientAccessDescription: Your account is linked to one doctor for MVP.
dashboard.requiresSetup: Your first-login setup is still pending. Finish it before accessing the rest of the app.
dashboard.onboardingComplete: Your onboarding is complete. Your current medication list is shown below, and you can log adherence directly from this dashboard.
dashboard.noActiveMedications: No active medications have been assigned yet.

errors.loadData: Unable to load patient dashboard data.

patientsPage.title: Patient roster
patientsPage.subtitle: Create patient accounts, manage assigned treatment tasks, and review recent history with safety highlighting in one place.
patientsPage.selectedPatientDescription: Medication assignment and adherence review always apply to the currently selected patient.
patientsPage.noActiveMedications: No active medications are recorded for this patient yet.
patientsPage.adherenceDescription: Review the latest patient-reported adherence records as structured entries instead of relying on memory during follow-up.
patientsPage.noAdherenceRecords: No adherence records have been submitted for this patient yet.

patientOnboarding.title: Create patient access
patientOnboarding.subtitle: Add a patient account and hand over the generated temporary password for the first sign-in.
patientOnboarding.temporaryPasswordHint: A temporary password is shown once after creation.
patientOnboarding.credentialsHelp: Share these credentials with {email}. The patient will be forced to set a permanent password after the first login.

patientRosterTable.title: Assigned patients
patientRosterTable.subtitle: Doctors can see only their own patients, along with onboarding and activation state.
patientRosterTable.empty: No patients yet. Create the first patient account above.

doctorMedicationForm.title: Assign current medication
doctorMedicationForm.subtitle: Record the current medication list for the selected patient and keep dosage instructions visible for future follow-up.
doctorMedicationForm.selectPatientFirst: Select a patient above to start medication tracking.

medicationList.title: Medication list
medicationList.subtitle: Active medications stay visible here so the current plan and adherence log always point to the same record.

adherenceForm.title: Log adherence
adherenceForm.subtitle: Submit whether a medication was taken as planned and add a note only when something changed.
adherenceForm.notePlaceholder: Optional context if the dose was missed or changed

sideEffectsForm.title: Report side effects
sideEffectsForm.subtitle: Share symptoms between visits so your doctor can quickly spot severe changes and follow up.

doctorQuestionnaireForm.title: Assign PHQ-9 or GAD-7
doctorQuestionnaireForm.subtitle: Create a pending questionnaire for the selected patient so they can complete it from their dashboard.

patientQuestionnaireList.title: Pending tasks
patientQuestionnaireList.subtitle: Complete assigned questionnaires to keep your doctor updated between visits.

PHQ-9.q1: Little interest or pleasure in doing things
PHQ-9.q3: Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much
PHQ-9.q4: Feeling tired or having little energy
PHQ-9.q8: Moving or speaking slowly or being fidgety/restless
PHQ-9.q9: Thoughts that you would be better off dead or of hurting yourself

GAD-7.q1: Feeling nervous, anxious, or on edge
GAD-7.q2: Not being able to stop or control worrying
GAD-7.q3: Worrying too much about different things
GAD-7.q5: Being so restless that it is hard to sit still
GAD-7.q7: Feeling afraid as if something awful might happen

questionnairePage.title: Complete questionnaire
questionnairePage.subtitle: Answer each item using a score from 0 to 3, then submit the form once.

patientSummary.title: Recent patient summary
patientSummary.subtitle: One review surface for recent questionnaires, adherence, and side effects with explicit safety highlighting.
patientSummary.noSafetyFlags: No active safety flags from recent history.
